articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The government announced the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing green hydrogen hubs.
• Key objectives include promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolysers, developing export capabilities, and creating a robust supply chain.
• The mission aims to make India a global leader in green hydrogen production and utilization, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization.
• Financial incentives, R&D grants, and policy support for infrastructure development are central to this phase.
• This initiative is expected to attract substantial domestic and international investment, creating numerous job opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Aims for 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonne) annual green hydrogen production by 2030.
SIGHT programme provides financial incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic benefits of scaling up green hydrogen production for India's energy independence.
Analyze the environmental impact and India's commitment to climate change mitigation through green hydrogen.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities in indigenous electrolyser manufacturing.
Evaluate the role of policy support and financial incentives in achieving the mission's objectives.
Assess India's potential to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Rules Amendments
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced it would hear a batch of petitions challenging certain amendments proposed to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) rules.
• Concerns raised include potential impacts on free speech, government surveillance powers, and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• Civil society groups and privacy advocates argue that some provisions could dilute the intent of protecting individual privacy.
• The government maintains that the amendments are necessary to streamline implementation and address evolving digital challenges.
• The outcome of these hearings could significantly shape India's data governance framework and digital rights.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act passed in 2023.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution ensures the Right to Privacy.
Justice K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017) case declared privacy a fundamental right.
Data Protection Board of India is established under the DPDP Act.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the balance between national security/governance and individual privacy in the context of digital data protection laws.
Discuss the potential implications of the proposed amendments on freedom of speech and expression in the digital sphere.
Evaluate the independence and effectiveness of the Data Protection Board under the current framework.
Examine global best practices in data protection and compare them with India's approach.
Suggest measures to strengthen the DPDP Act to ensure robust protection of citizens' data.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel on a legal document with a background of digital data streams.
articles.trivia: The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), enacted in 2018, is considered one of the strictest privacy and security laws in the world.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

articles.headline: Veteran Politician B. Subbayya Shetty Passes Away at 91
articles.explainer:
• B. Subbayya Shetty, a prominent veteran politician known for his public service, passed away at the age of 91.
• Shetty had a long and distinguished career, serving multiple terms as a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) in Karnataka.
• He was recognized for his contributions to social welfare, education, and rural development in his constituency.
• Shetty's political journey spanned several decades, witnessing significant transformations in Indian politics.
• His demise marks the end of an era for many who remember his dedication to grassroots issues.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: B. Subbayya Shetty served as an MLA in Karnataka.
MLAs are members of a State Legislative Assembly.
The term of an MLA is typically five years.
Karnataka is one of the six states with a bicameral legislature (Legislative Assembly and Legislative Council).
The minimum age to be an MLA is 25 years.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Final Stage
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their crucial final stage, with both sides expressing optimism for a swift conclusion.
• Key areas of discussion include tariffs on goods, services trade (particularly financial services and IT), investment protection, and intellectual property rights.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses in both countries.
• Challenges remain in areas like market access for certain agricultural products and professional services.
• A successful agreement is expected to strengthen India's strategic partnership with the UK post-Brexit.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: FTA aims to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
India is negotiating FTAs with several countries/blocs, including the UK, EU, and Canada.
The UK is a major trading partner for India, especially in services.
Post-Brexit, the UK is seeking new trade agreements globally.
Key sectors for India in the FTA include textiles, pharmaceuticals, and IT services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges for India from a comprehensive FTA with the UK.
Discuss how the India-UK FTA could impact India's broader trade policy and its 'Act East' and 'Look West' strategies.
Examine the geopolitical significance of strengthening economic ties between India and the UK.
Evaluate the implications of the FTA on specific Indian industries, such as agriculture, manufacturing, and services.
Suggest strategies for India to leverage the FTA for greater market access and investment inflows.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom superimposed over a graph showing economic growth.
articles.trivia: The UK was India's 6th largest trading partner in goods in 2022-23.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Evolving Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, opting for a cautious approach.
• The decision was influenced by evolving domestic and global inflationary pressures, particularly from food and energy prices.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest that future rate actions will be data-dependent, closely monitoring core inflation and global economic trends.
• This stance aims to provide stability to the financial markets and manage expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a 6-member body responsible for setting policy rates.
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target for the RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the effectiveness of the current monetary policy tools in managing inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the impact of global factors, such as crude oil prices and geopolitical events, on India's inflation trajectory.
Evaluate the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy for macroeconomic stability.
Critically assess the data-dependent approach of the MPC and its implications for future rate decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with Indian currency notes in the background.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India Hosts Multilateral Dialogue on Maritime Security in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a high-level multilateral dialogue focusing on enhancing maritime security and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Representatives from key regional and extra-regional powers participated, discussing challenges like piracy, illegal fishing, and freedom of navigation.
• The dialogue emphasized the need for a rules-based international order and collaborative approaches to ensure a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions also covered capacity building, information sharing, and joint exercises among naval forces.
• This initiative underscores India's growing role as a net security provider and a proponent of regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Indo-Pacific concept includes the Indian Ocean and the Western and Central Pacific Ocean.
India's SAGAR doctrine focuses on maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) also focuses on Indo-Pacific maritime security.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) provides the legal framework for maritime activities.
Piracy in the Gulf of Aden and illegal fishing are major challenges in the region.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic imperatives and evolving role in ensuring maritime security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the major challenges to maritime security in the Indo-Pacific and the collaborative solutions being pursued.
Examine the significance of a rules-based international order for stability and prosperity in the region.
Evaluate the impact of great power competition on maritime dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
Suggest policy recommendations for India to further strengthen its position as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships patrolling in open waters with flags of various Indo-Pacific nations.
articles.trivia: The Malacca Strait, connecting the Indian Ocean and the Pacific Ocean, is one of the world's most important shipping lanes.

articles.headline: ISRO Prepares for Second Uncrewed Gaganyaan Test Flight
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced preparations for the second uncrewed test flight of its Gaganyaan mission, designated 'TV-D2'.
• This flight is crucial for validating the crew escape system and other critical technologies required for human spaceflight.
• The mission aims to test the safety and reliability of the spacecraft's systems under various simulated conditions.
• Data gathered from TV-D2 will inform further design refinements and operational procedures for the crewed mission.
• Gaganyaan is India's ambitious human spaceflight program, aiming to send astronauts to low Earth orbit.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight program.
The mission aims to send a 3-member crew to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.
LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk-III) is the launch vehicle for Gaganyaan.
The crew escape system is designed to quickly pull the crew module away from the launch vehicle in case of an anomaly.
Vyommitra is the humanoid robot developed by ISRO for uncrewed Gaganyaan missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan) for its scientific and technological capabilities.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing a reliable crew escape system for human space missions.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits and spin-off technologies expected from the Gaganyaan mission.
Examine India's position in global space exploration and collaboration opportunities with other space agencies.
Critically assess the ethical considerations and risks associated with human spaceflight programs.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of the Gaganyaan crew module separating from the launch vehicle during a test.
articles.trivia: The first Indian in space was Rakesh Sharma, who flew aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11 in 1984.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing is showing significant progress, attracting major investments.
• Several companies have commenced or are nearing commencement of production facilities under the scheme.
• This initiative is crucial for India's push towards electric mobility and renewable energy storage solutions.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's reliance on imports for battery technology and establish a robust domestic manufacturing ecosystem.
• It is expected to create a competitive global supply chain for ACC batteries, fostering innovation and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme for ACC batteries was approved in 2021.
It aims to achieve 50 GWh of ACC manufacturing capacity.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
The scheme is designed to attract investments in cutting-edge battery technology.
India is promoting EVs to reduce carbon emissions and oil imports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's electric mobility and renewable energy targets.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing a domestic supply chain for advanced battery technologies.
Evaluate the economic impact of the PLI scheme on job creation, investment, and technological indigenization.
Examine the role of ACC batteries in India's broader energy transition strategy and climate commitments.
Suggest policy measures to further accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles and energy storage solutions in India.
articles.imageDescription: An array of advanced chemistry cells (batteries) with the 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: The global market for lithium-ion batteries is projected to grow significantly, driven by the demand from electric vehicles.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Economic and Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to deepening cooperation across economic and security domains.
• Discussions focused on enhancing bilateral trade, investment, infrastructure development, and collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing to bolster defense and security ties.
• The dialogue also covered regional and global issues, including climate change, counter-terrorism, and UN reforms.
• This partnership is considered a cornerstone of stability and prosperity in Asia and beyond.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad group, along with the US and Australia.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries share concerns about maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
India's 'Act East Policy' aligns with Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision.
Defense cooperation includes joint military exercises like 'Dharma Guardian'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in the contemporary geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the multifaceted aspects of India-Japan cooperation, covering economic, security, and technological dimensions.
Examine how the India-Japan partnership contributes to regional stability and a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in further deepening bilateral ties, particularly in critical and emerging technologies.
Suggest ways India and Japan can collaborate on global issues such as climate change, UN reforms, and sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is the only country with which India has a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers).

articles.headline: Government Launches National Quantum Computing Research Program
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology announced the launch of a comprehensive National Quantum Computing Research Program.
• The program aims to accelerate R&D in quantum computing, quantum communication, and quantum sensing technologies.
• It involves collaboration between academic institutions, industry, and government research labs to build a robust quantum ecosystem.
• Funding will be provided for cutting-edge research, infrastructure development, and talent training in quantum science.
• This initiative positions India to be at the forefront of the next generation of computing and technological advancements.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
tags: National Mission on Quantum Technologies and Applications (NMQTA)
prelimsPoints: National Mission on Quantum Technologies and Applications (NMQTA) was launched in 2020.
Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics like superposition and entanglement.
Qubit is the basic unit of information in quantum computing.
Potential applications include drug discovery, materials science, cryptography, and AI.
India aims to develop indigenous capabilities in quantum technologies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of quantum computing and its various applications across different sectors.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in developing a robust quantum technology ecosystem.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing and communication.
Evaluate the role of government funding and public-private partnerships in fostering quantum research and development.
Suggest strategies for India to bridge the talent gap and become a global leader in quantum technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image representing quantum computing with interconnected nodes and light beams.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits, which can represent 0, 1, or both simultaneously.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Urban Waste Management Announced
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework for sustainable urban waste management.
• The policy emphasizes a circular economy approach, focusing on waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and resource recovery.
• It mandates greater public participation, extended producer responsibility, and adoption of advanced waste processing technologies.
• Financial incentives for urban local bodies and private sector involvement in waste-to-energy projects are also included.
• The framework aims to tackle the growing challenge of urban waste and improve public health and environmental quality.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 are the primary legal framework.
Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) focuses on urban sanitation and waste management.
Circular economy aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible.
Waste segregation at source is a key principle.
Waste-to-energy plants convert non-recyclable waste into electricity or heat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges in urban solid waste management in India and how the new policy framework addresses them.
Analyze the concept of a circular economy in the context of waste management and its potential benefits for India.
Evaluate the role of technology and public-private partnerships in achieving sustainable waste management goals.
Examine the socio-economic and environmental impacts of improper waste disposal in urban areas.
Suggest innovative approaches and behavioral changes necessary for effective implementation of the new policy.
articles.imageDescription: Workers sorting waste at a modern waste processing facility.
articles.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

articles.headline: Efforts Intensify to Conserve Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot
articles.explainer:
• Government and environmental organizations have intensified efforts to conserve the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats.
• New initiatives focus on protecting endangered species, restoring degraded forest areas, and promoting sustainable livelihoods for local communities.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, faces threats from deforestation, mining, and climate change.
• Collaborative projects involve local communities in conservation efforts through eco-tourism and sustainable agriculture practices.
• Research is ongoing to understand the impact of climate change on the region's flora and fauna.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is one of the 8 hottest biodiversity hotspots in the world.
It runs parallel to the western coast of the Indian peninsula.
Home to numerous endemic species of plants and animals.
UNESCO declared 39 sites within the Western Ghats as World Heritage Sites.
Major rivers like Godavari, Krishna, and Kaveri originate in the Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and its role in India's monsoon system.
Analyze the major threats to the Western Ghats ecosystem and the challenges in implementing conservation measures.
Evaluate the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees regarding the Western Ghats and their implications.
Examine the importance of involving local communities in conservation efforts and promoting sustainable development in the region.
Suggest integrated policy approaches to balance development needs with environmental protection in ecologically sensitive areas.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with misty mountains and diverse flora.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Along Northern Frontiers Accelerated
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated development of critical infrastructure along India's northern and northeastern borders.
• Projects include construction of new roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced communication networks to enhance troop mobility and logistics.
• This strategic push aims to improve connectivity to remote border areas and strengthen India's defensive capabilities.
• Emphasis is also on using advanced construction techniques suitable for challenging mountainous terrains.
• The move is seen as a response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and ensures robust border management.
gsSubTopics: Border areas and their management in security challenges and their management.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border infrastructure.
The Line of Actual Control (LAC) is the de facto border between India and China.
Daporijo bridge in Arunachal Pradesh and Atal Tunnel in Himachal Pradesh are key border infrastructure projects.
Infrastructure development is crucial for rapid troop deployment and supply lines.
The Border Area Development Programme (BADP) also contributes to infrastructure in border regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly along its northern frontiers.
Analyze the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, mountainous, and remote border areas.
Evaluate the impact of enhanced border connectivity on the socio-economic development of border populations.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India's accelerated border infrastructure development in the context of regional security dynamics.
Suggest measures to ensure the long-term sustainability and resilience of border infrastructure projects.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed strategic road winding through mountainous terrain in a border region.
articles.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, formerly known as Rohtang Tunnel, is the longest highway single-tube tunnel above 10,000 feet (3,000 m) in the world.

articles.headline: 'Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0' Focuses on Adolescent Health and Anaemia Eradication
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development announced an enhanced focus on adolescent health and anaemia eradication under 'Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0'.
• New initiatives include targeted nutritional interventions, health education campaigns, and improved access to iron and folic acid supplementation for adolescents.
• The program aims to address the high prevalence of anaemia among adolescent girls and improve their overall health outcomes.
• Collaboration with health and education departments is being strengthened to ensure comprehensive outreach.
• This push is critical for breaking the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition and improving future maternal and child health.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) launched in 2018.
Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0 subsumes the supplementary nutrition program and Poshan Abhiyaan.
Anaemia is a condition where blood lacks healthy red blood cells.
Adolescent girls are a vulnerable group for anaemia.
The program aims to reduce stunting, undernutrition, anaemia, and low birth weight.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges of malnutrition and anaemia in India, particularly among adolescents.
Analyze the strategies and interventions adopted under Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0 to improve adolescent health and eradicate anaemia.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inter-sectoral convergence and community participation in achieving nutrition targets.
Examine the long-term socio-economic impacts of addressing adolescent anaemia on human capital development.
Suggest policy recommendations to further strengthen nutritional outcomes and public health for vulnerable groups.
articles.imageDescription: Group of adolescent girls participating in a health awareness program.
articles.trivia: Anaemia is a global public health problem, affecting over 1.62 billion people worldwide, with adolescent girls and women of reproductive age being particularly susceptible.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Unveils New Digital Literacy Initiative for Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education launched a new national digital literacy initiative specifically targeting rural populations.
• The program aims to bridge the digital divide by providing foundational digital skills and internet awareness to underserved communities.
• It will leverage common service centers (CSCs), local educational institutions, and digital volunteers for training.
• The curriculum covers basic computer operations, internet usage, online safety, and access to government digital services.
• This initiative is crucial for empowering rural citizens, enhancing their participation in the digital economy, and promoting inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Digital India programme was launched in 2015.
Common Service Centers (CSCs) are access points for delivery of essential public utility services.
Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is a previous digital literacy scheme.
Digital literacy is the ability to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information using digital technologies.
Bridging the digital divide is essential for equitable development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of digital literacy in empowering rural populations and fostering inclusive growth in India.
Analyze the challenges in achieving universal digital literacy in rural areas and how the new initiative plans to address them.
Evaluate the role of Common Service Centers (CSCs) and local institutions in the successful implementation of such programs.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits of enhanced digital literacy, including access to education, healthcare, and financial services.
Suggest innovative approaches and public-private partnerships to sustain and scale up digital literacy efforts across the country.
articles.imageDescription: A group of rural villagers learning basic computer skills in a community center.
articles.trivia: India has the world's second-largest internet user base, but a significant portion of its rural population still lacks digital literacy.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Debates Amendments to Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha commenced debate on proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act, 2002.
• The amendments aim to improve governance, enhance transparency, and strengthen the financial health of cooperative societies.
• Key provisions include electoral reforms, measures for financial discipline, and a framework for rehabilitation of sick cooperative societies.
• Critics raised concerns about potential over-centralization of power and interference in the autonomy of cooperatives.
• The government asserts that the amendments are necessary to promote ease of doing business and ensure democratic functioning.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011, gave constitutional status to cooperative societies.
Article 19(1)(c) guarantees the right to form cooperative societies.
Article 43B promotes voluntary formation, autonomous functioning, democratic control, and professional management of cooperative societies.
Multi-State Cooperative Societies operate in more than one state.
The Ministry of Cooperation was formed in 2021.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of cooperative societies in India's socio-economic development, particularly in rural areas.
Analyze the proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act and their potential impact on the autonomy and functioning of cooperatives.
Evaluate the challenges faced by cooperative societies in India, including governance issues, financial irregularities, and lack of professional management.
Examine the role of the Ministry of Cooperation in strengthening the cooperative movement and addressing its challenges.
Suggest measures to strike a balance between ensuring good governance and preserving the democratic and autonomous character of cooperative societies.
articles.imageDescription: Parliament House in New Delhi, symbolizing legislative debate.
articles.trivia: India has one of the largest networks of cooperatives in the world, with over 8.5 lakh cooperative societies.

articles.headline: India Pledges Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund
articles.explainer:
• India announced significant financial and technical support for a newly proposed Global South Climate Adaptation Fund.
• The fund aims to assist developing nations, particularly Least Developed Countries (LDCs) and Small Island Developing States (SIDS), in building resilience to climate change impacts.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate finance.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to climate action and its leadership role among developing countries.
• This move is expected to galvanize greater international cooperation and resource mobilization for climate adaptation.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: CBDR-RC is a principle under the UNFCCC.
Climate adaptation refers to adjustments in natural or human systems in response to actual or expected climatic stimuli.
Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a global fund to support developing countries in climate action.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South on climate change issues.
Analyze the importance of climate adaptation finance for developing countries and the challenges in its mobilization.
Evaluate the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate action.
Examine the potential impact of India's support for the Global South Climate Adaptation Fund on international climate negotiations.
Suggest strategies for strengthening climate resilience in vulnerable nations through international cooperation and technology transfer.
articles.imageDescription: World map highlighting countries of the Global South, symbolizing climate cooperation.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

highlights: India Unveils Ambitious Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission to boost production and establish hubs.
Supreme Court to hear challenges to amendments in Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, impacting privacy and governance.
Veteran Politician B. Subbayya Shetty passes away at 91, remembered for his long public service in Karnataka.
India-UK Free Trade Agreement negotiations enter crucial final stage, aiming to significantly boost bilateral trade.
ISRO prepares for second uncrewed Gaganyaan test flight, 'TV-D2', crucial for human spaceflight mission validation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims for 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production annually by 2030.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme provides financial incentives for electrolyser manufacturing.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using non-renewable energy sources.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT annual green hydrogen production by 2030. Statement 2 is correct. The SIGHT programme is a key component providing financial incentives. Statement 3 is incorrect. Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources for electrolysis, not non-renewable ones.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the policy rates in India, primarily focusing on maintaining price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a 6-member body specifically tasked with determining the policy interest rates (like the repo rate) to achieve the primary objective of price stability while supporting growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act):
1. It was passed in 2023.
2. The Right to Privacy is explicitly mentioned as a fundamental right under Article 14 of the Indian Constitution.
3. The Data Protection Board of India is established under this Act.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the DPDP Act was passed in 2023. Statement 2 is incorrect; the Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 by the Supreme Court in the K.S. Puttaswamy case, not explicitly under Article 14. Statement 3 is correct; the Data Protection Board of India is a key institutional mechanism established by the Act.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements about the Western Ghats is NOT correct?
options: (a) It is recognized as one of the world's eight hottest biodiversity hotspots.
(b) It is older than the Himalayan mountain range.
(c) Major rivers like Godavari, Krishna, and Kaveri originate in the Western Ghats.
(d) It is entirely located within the state of Kerala.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) It is entirely located within the state of Kerala.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements (a), (b), and (c) are correct. Statement (d) is incorrect. The Western Ghats extend through six Indian states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's human spaceflight program, Gaganyaan, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The mission aims to send astronauts to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
2. The Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM3) is the designated launch vehicle for Gaganyaan.
3. Vyommitra is the name of the first Indian astronaut selected for the mission.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Gaganyaan aims to send a crew to LEO. Statement 2 is correct; LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk-III) is the launch vehicle. Statement 3 is incorrect; Vyommitra is the humanoid robot developed by ISRO for uncrewed Gaganyaan missions, not an astronaut.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. Discuss its potential to transform India's energy landscape and its role in achieving net-zero emissions, while also highlighting the associated challenges. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the mission's objectives (5 MMT by 2030, net-zero by 2070). Discuss potential benefits: energy security, reduced imports, decarbonization, economic growth, job creation, global leadership. Highlight challenges: high production costs, need for massive renewable energy infrastructure, water availability, technological hurdles (electrolyser efficiency), storage and transportation, policy implementation. Conclude with a balanced perspective on its transformative potential.

mainsMocks.question: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is poised to significantly impact India's trade and strategic relations. Discuss the key opportunities and challenges for India in concluding and implementing this FTA. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly state the context of India-UK FTA. Opportunities: enhanced market access for goods and services, increased investment, technology transfer, strengthened strategic partnership post-Brexit. Challenges: tariff barriers on sensitive products (agriculture, automotive), intellectual property rights, data localization, visa issues for professionals, domestic industry protection. Conclude on the need for a balanced and mutually beneficial agreement.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) aims to safeguard individual privacy in the digital age. In light of recent proposed amendments, critically examine the balance between national security concerns, government access to data, and the fundamental right to privacy. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce DPDP Act and the right to privacy (Puttaswamy judgment). Explain the tension between national security/governance needs (e.g., surveillance, data access for law enforcement) and individual privacy. Discuss concerns raised about proposed amendments (e.g., Data Protection Board independence, exemptions for government agencies, impact on free speech). Analyze how these might dilute privacy safeguards. Conclude with suggestions for a robust, rights-respecting data governance framework.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production.
B. Subbayya Shetty, a veteran politician, served as a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) in Karnataka.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) was passed in India in the year 2023.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI is a 6-member body.
The Indo-Pacific region includes the Indian Ocean and the Western and Central Pacific Ocean.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight program, using the LVM3 launch vehicle.
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing aims for 50 GWh capacity.
The Western Ghats are a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's 8 hottest biodiversity hotspots.
The 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011, gave constitutional status to cooperative societies.
The principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) is under UNFCCC.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Rules Amendments
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court announced it would hear a batch of petitions challenging certain amendments proposed to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) rules.
• Concerns raised include potential impacts on free speech, government surveillance powers, and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• Civil society groups and privacy advocates argue that some provisions could dilute the intent of protecting individual privacy.
• The government maintains that the amendments are necessary to streamline implementation and address evolving digital challenges.
• The outcome of these hearings could significantly shape India's data governance framework and digital rights.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act passed in 2023.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution ensures the Right to Privacy.
Justice K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017) case declared privacy a fundamental right.
Data Protection Board of India is established under the DPDP Act.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the balance between national security/governance and individual privacy in the context of digital data protection laws.
Discuss the potential implications of the proposed amendments on freedom of speech and expression in the digital sphere.
Evaluate the independence and effectiveness of the Data Protection Board under the current framework.
Examine global best practices in data protection and compare them with India's approach.
Suggest measures to strengthen the DPDP Act to ensure robust protection of citizens' data.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel on a legal document with a background of digital data streams.
GS2.trivia: The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), enacted in 2018, is considered one of the strictest privacy and security laws in the world.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician B. Subbayya Shetty Passes Away at 91
GS2.explainer:
• B. Subbayya Shetty, a prominent veteran politician known for his public service, passed away at the age of 91.
• Shetty had a long and distinguished career, serving multiple terms as a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) in Karnataka.
• He was recognized for his contributions to social welfare, education, and rural development in his constituency.
• Shetty's political journey spanned several decades, witnessing significant transformations in Indian politics.
• His demise marks the end of an era for many who remember his dedication to grassroots issues.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: B. Subbayya Shetty served as an MLA in Karnataka.
MLAs are members of a State Legislative Assembly.
The term of an MLA is typically five years.
Karnataka is one of the six states with a bicameral legislature (Legislative Assembly and Legislative Council).
The minimum age to be an MLA is 25 years.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

GS2.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Crucial Final Stage
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their crucial final stage, with both sides expressing optimism for a swift conclusion.
• Key areas of discussion include tariffs on goods, services trade (particularly financial services and IT), investment protection, and intellectual property rights.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses in both countries.
• Challenges remain in areas like market access for certain agricultural products and professional services.
• A successful agreement is expected to strengthen India's strategic partnership with the UK post-Brexit.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: FTA aims to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
India is negotiating FTAs with several countries/blocs, including the UK, EU, and Canada.
The UK is a major trading partner for India, especially in services.
Post-Brexit, the UK is seeking new trade agreements globally.
Key sectors for India in the FTA include textiles, pharmaceuticals, and IT services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and challenges for India from a comprehensive FTA with the UK.
Discuss how the India-UK FTA could impact India's broader trade policy and its 'Act East' and 'Look West' strategies.
Examine the geopolitical significance of strengthening economic ties between India and the UK.
Evaluate the implications of the FTA on specific Indian industries, such as agriculture, manufacturing, and services.
Suggest strategies for India to leverage the FTA for greater market access and investment inflows.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom superimposed over a graph showing economic growth.
GS2.trivia: The UK was India's 6th largest trading partner in goods in 2022-23.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Multilateral Dialogue on Maritime Security in Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a high-level multilateral dialogue focusing on enhancing maritime security and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Representatives from key regional and extra-regional powers participated, discussing challenges like piracy, illegal fishing, and freedom of navigation.
• The dialogue emphasized the need for a rules-based international order and collaborative approaches to ensure a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions also covered capacity building, information sharing, and joint exercises among naval forces.
• This initiative underscores India's growing role as a net security provider and a proponent of regional stability.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Indo-Pacific concept includes the Indian Ocean and the Western and Central Pacific Ocean.
India's SAGAR doctrine focuses on maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) also focuses on Indo-Pacific maritime security.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) provides the legal framework for maritime activities.
Piracy in the Gulf of Aden and illegal fishing are major challenges in the region.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic imperatives and evolving role in ensuring maritime security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the major challenges to maritime security in the Indo-Pacific and the collaborative solutions being pursued.
Examine the significance of a rules-based international order for stability and prosperity in the region.
Evaluate the impact of great power competition on maritime dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
Suggest policy recommendations for India to further strengthen its position as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships patrolling in open waters with flags of various Indo-Pacific nations.
GS2.trivia: The Malacca Strait, connecting the Indian Ocean and the Pacific Ocean, is one of the world's most important shipping lanes.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Economic and Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to deepening cooperation across economic and security domains.
• Discussions focused on enhancing bilateral trade, investment, infrastructure development, and collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing to bolster defense and security ties.
• The dialogue also covered regional and global issues, including climate change, counter-terrorism, and UN reforms.
• This partnership is considered a cornerstone of stability and prosperity in Asia and beyond.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad group, along with the US and Australia.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries share concerns about maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
India's 'Act East Policy' aligns with Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision.
Defense cooperation includes joint military exercises like 'Dharma Guardian'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in the contemporary geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the multifaceted aspects of India-Japan cooperation, covering economic, security, and technological dimensions.
Examine how the India-Japan partnership contributes to regional stability and a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in further deepening bilateral ties, particularly in critical and emerging technologies.
Suggest ways India and Japan can collaborate on global issues such as climate change, UN reforms, and sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is the only country with which India has a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers).

GS2.headline: 'Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0' Focuses on Adolescent Health and Anaemia Eradication
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development announced an enhanced focus on adolescent health and anaemia eradication under 'Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0'.
• New initiatives include targeted nutritional interventions, health education campaigns, and improved access to iron and folic acid supplementation for adolescents.
• The program aims to address the high prevalence of anaemia among adolescent girls and improve their overall health outcomes.
• Collaboration with health and education departments is being strengthened to ensure comprehensive outreach.
• This push is critical for breaking the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition and improving future maternal and child health.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) launched in 2018.
Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0 subsumes the supplementary nutrition program and Poshan Abhiyaan.
Anaemia is a condition where blood lacks healthy red blood cells.
Adolescent girls are a vulnerable group for anaemia.
The program aims to reduce stunting, undernutrition, anaemia, and low birth weight.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges of malnutrition and anaemia in India, particularly among adolescents.
Analyze the strategies and interventions adopted under Poshan Abhiyaan 2.0 to improve adolescent health and eradicate anaemia.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inter-sectoral convergence and community participation in achieving nutrition targets.
Examine the long-term socio-economic impacts of addressing adolescent anaemia on human capital development.
Suggest policy recommendations to further strengthen nutritional outcomes and public health for vulnerable groups.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of adolescent girls participating in a health awareness program.
GS2.trivia: Anaemia is a global public health problem, affecting over 1.62 billion people worldwide, with adolescent girls and women of reproductive age being particularly susceptible.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Unveils New Digital Literacy Initiative for Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education launched a new national digital literacy initiative specifically targeting rural populations.
• The program aims to bridge the digital divide by providing foundational digital skills and internet awareness to underserved communities.
• It will leverage common service centers (CSCs), local educational institutions, and digital volunteers for training.
• The curriculum covers basic computer operations, internet usage, online safety, and access to government digital services.
• This initiative is crucial for empowering rural citizens, enhancing their participation in the digital economy, and promoting inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Digital India programme was launched in 2015.
Common Service Centers (CSCs) are access points for delivery of essential public utility services.
Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is a previous digital literacy scheme.
Digital literacy is the ability to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information using digital technologies.
Bridging the digital divide is essential for equitable development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of digital literacy in empowering rural populations and fostering inclusive growth in India.
Analyze the challenges in achieving universal digital literacy in rural areas and how the new initiative plans to address them.
Evaluate the role of Common Service Centers (CSCs) and local institutions in the successful implementation of such programs.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits of enhanced digital literacy, including access to education, healthcare, and financial services.
Suggest innovative approaches and public-private partnerships to sustain and scale up digital literacy efforts across the country.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of rural villagers learning basic computer skills in a community center.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's second-largest internet user base, but a significant portion of its rural population still lacks digital literacy.

GS2.headline: Lok Sabha Debates Amendments to Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha commenced debate on proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act, 2002.
• The amendments aim to improve governance, enhance transparency, and strengthen the financial health of cooperative societies.
• Key provisions include electoral reforms, measures for financial discipline, and a framework for rehabilitation of sick cooperative societies.
• Critics raised concerns about potential over-centralization of power and interference in the autonomy of cooperatives.
• The government asserts that the amendments are necessary to promote ease of doing business and ensure democratic functioning.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011, gave constitutional status to cooperative societies.
Article 19(1)(c) guarantees the right to form cooperative societies.
Article 43B promotes voluntary formation, autonomous functioning, democratic control, and professional management of cooperative societies.
Multi-State Cooperative Societies operate in more than one state.
The Ministry of Cooperation was formed in 2021.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of cooperative societies in India's socio-economic development, particularly in rural areas.
Analyze the proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act and their potential impact on the autonomy and functioning of cooperatives.
Evaluate the challenges faced by cooperative societies in India, including governance issues, financial irregularities, and lack of professional management.
Examine the role of the Ministry of Cooperation in strengthening the cooperative movement and addressing its challenges.
Suggest measures to strike a balance between ensuring good governance and preserving the democratic and autonomous character of cooperative societies.
GS2.imageDescription: Parliament House in New Delhi, symbolizing legislative debate.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the largest networks of cooperatives in the world, with over 8.5 lakh cooperative societies.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund
GS2.explainer:
• India announced significant financial and technical support for a newly proposed Global South Climate Adaptation Fund.
• The fund aims to assist developing nations, particularly Least Developed Countries (LDCs) and Small Island Developing States (SIDS), in building resilience to climate change impacts.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate finance.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to climate action and its leadership role among developing countries.
• This move is expected to galvanize greater international cooperation and resource mobilization for climate adaptation.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: CBDR-RC is a principle under the UNFCCC.
Climate adaptation refers to adjustments in natural or human systems in response to actual or expected climatic stimuli.
Global South refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a global fund to support developing countries in climate action.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South on climate change issues.
Analyze the importance of climate adaptation finance for developing countries and the challenges in its mobilization.
Evaluate the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate action.
Examine the potential impact of India's support for the Global South Climate Adaptation Fund on international climate negotiations.
Suggest strategies for strengthening climate resilience in vulnerable nations through international cooperation and technology transfer.
GS2.imageDescription: World map highlighting countries of the Global South, symbolizing climate cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing green hydrogen hubs.
• Key objectives include promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolysers, developing export capabilities, and creating a robust supply chain.
• The mission aims to make India a global leader in green hydrogen production and utilization, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization.
• Financial incentives, R&D grants, and policy support for infrastructure development are central to this phase.
• This initiative is expected to attract substantial domestic and international investment, creating numerous job opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Aims for 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonne) annual green hydrogen production by 2030.
SIGHT programme provides financial incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic benefits of scaling up green hydrogen production for India's energy independence.
Analyze the environmental impact and India's commitment to climate change mitigation through green hydrogen.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities in indigenous electrolyser manufacturing.
Evaluate the role of policy support and financial incentives in achieving the mission's objectives.
Assess India's potential to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Evolving Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, opting for a cautious approach.
• The decision was influenced by evolving domestic and global inflationary pressures, particularly from food and energy prices.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest that future rate actions will be data-dependent, closely monitoring core inflation and global economic trends.
• This stance aims to provide stability to the financial markets and manage expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a 6-member body responsible for setting policy rates.
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The current inflation target for the RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the effectiveness of the current monetary policy tools in managing inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the impact of global factors, such as crude oil prices and geopolitical events, on India's inflation trajectory.
Evaluate the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy for macroeconomic stability.
Critically assess the data-dependent approach of the MPC and its implications for future rate decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with Indian currency notes in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Prepares for Second Uncrewed Gaganyaan Test Flight
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced preparations for the second uncrewed test flight of its Gaganyaan mission, designated 'TV-D2'.
• This flight is crucial for validating the crew escape system and other critical technologies required for human spaceflight.
• The mission aims to test the safety and reliability of the spacecraft's systems under various simulated conditions.
• Data gathered from TV-D2 will inform further design refinements and operational procedures for the crewed mission.
• Gaganyaan is India's ambitious human spaceflight program, aiming to send astronauts to low Earth orbit.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight program.
The mission aims to send a 3-member crew to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.
LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk-III) is the launch vehicle for Gaganyaan.
The crew escape system is designed to quickly pull the crew module away from the launch vehicle in case of an anomaly.
Vyommitra is the humanoid robot developed by ISRO for uncrewed Gaganyaan missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan) for its scientific and technological capabilities.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing a reliable crew escape system for human space missions.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits and spin-off technologies expected from the Gaganyaan mission.
Examine India's position in global space exploration and collaboration opportunities with other space agencies.
Critically assess the ethical considerations and risks associated with human spaceflight programs.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of the Gaganyaan crew module separating from the launch vehicle during a test.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian in space was Rakesh Sharma, who flew aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11 in 1984.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing is showing significant progress, attracting major investments.
• Several companies have commenced or are nearing commencement of production facilities under the scheme.
• This initiative is crucial for India's push towards electric mobility and renewable energy storage solutions.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's reliance on imports for battery technology and establish a robust domestic manufacturing ecosystem.
• It is expected to create a competitive global supply chain for ACC batteries, fostering innovation and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme for ACC batteries was approved in 2021.
It aims to achieve 50 GWh of ACC manufacturing capacity.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
The scheme is designed to attract investments in cutting-edge battery technology.
India is promoting EVs to reduce carbon emissions and oil imports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's electric mobility and renewable energy targets.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing a domestic supply chain for advanced battery technologies.
Evaluate the economic impact of the PLI scheme on job creation, investment, and technological indigenization.
Examine the role of ACC batteries in India's broader energy transition strategy and climate commitments.
Suggest policy measures to further accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles and energy storage solutions in India.
GS3.imageDescription: An array of advanced chemistry cells (batteries) with the 'Make in India' logo.
GS3.trivia: The global market for lithium-ion batteries is projected to grow significantly, driven by the demand from electric vehicles.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Quantum Computing Research Program
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology announced the launch of a comprehensive National Quantum Computing Research Program.
• The program aims to accelerate R&D in quantum computing, quantum communication, and quantum sensing technologies.
• It involves collaboration between academic institutions, industry, and government research labs to build a robust quantum ecosystem.
• Funding will be provided for cutting-edge research, infrastructure development, and talent training in quantum science.
• This initiative positions India to be at the forefront of the next generation of computing and technological advancements.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
tags: National Mission on Quantum Technologies and Applications (NMQTA)
prelimsPoints: National Mission on Quantum Technologies and Applications (NMQTA) was launched in 2020.
Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics like superposition and entanglement.
Qubit is the basic unit of information in quantum computing.
Potential applications include drug discovery, materials science, cryptography, and AI.
India aims to develop indigenous capabilities in quantum technologies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of quantum computing and its various applications across different sectors.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in developing a robust quantum technology ecosystem.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing and communication.
Evaluate the role of government funding and public-private partnerships in fostering quantum research and development.
Suggest strategies for India to bridge the talent gap and become a global leader in quantum technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract image representing quantum computing with interconnected nodes and light beams.
GS3.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits, which can represent 0, 1, or both simultaneously.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Urban Waste Management Announced
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework for sustainable urban waste management.
• The policy emphasizes a circular economy approach, focusing on waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and resource recovery.
• It mandates greater public participation, extended producer responsibility, and adoption of advanced waste processing technologies.
• Financial incentives for urban local bodies and private sector involvement in waste-to-energy projects are also included.
• The framework aims to tackle the growing challenge of urban waste and improve public health and environmental quality.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 are the primary legal framework.
Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) focuses on urban sanitation and waste management.
Circular economy aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible.
Waste segregation at source is a key principle.
Waste-to-energy plants convert non-recyclable waste into electricity or heat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges in urban solid waste management in India and how the new policy framework addresses them.
Analyze the concept of a circular economy in the context of waste management and its potential benefits for India.
Evaluate the role of technology and public-private partnerships in achieving sustainable waste management goals.
Examine the socio-economic and environmental impacts of improper waste disposal in urban areas.
Suggest innovative approaches and behavioral changes necessary for effective implementation of the new policy.
GS3.imageDescription: Workers sorting waste at a modern waste processing facility.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

GS3.headline: Efforts Intensify to Conserve Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot
GS3.explainer:
• Government and environmental organizations have intensified efforts to conserve the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats.
• New initiatives focus on protecting endangered species, restoring degraded forest areas, and promoting sustainable livelihoods for local communities.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, faces threats from deforestation, mining, and climate change.
• Collaborative projects involve local communities in conservation efforts through eco-tourism and sustainable agriculture practices.
• Research is ongoing to understand the impact of climate change on the region's flora and fauna.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is one of the 8 hottest biodiversity hotspots in the world.
It runs parallel to the western coast of the Indian peninsula.
Home to numerous endemic species of plants and animals.
UNESCO declared 39 sites within the Western Ghats as World Heritage Sites.
Major rivers like Godavari, Krishna, and Kaveri originate in the Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and its role in India's monsoon system.
Analyze the major threats to the Western Ghats ecosystem and the challenges in implementing conservation measures.
Evaluate the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees regarding the Western Ghats and their implications.
Examine the importance of involving local communities in conservation efforts and promoting sustainable development in the region.
Suggest integrated policy approaches to balance development needs with environmental protection in ecologically sensitive areas.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with misty mountains and diverse flora.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Along Northern Frontiers Accelerated
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated development of critical infrastructure along India's northern and northeastern borders.
• Projects include construction of new roads, bridges, tunnels, and advanced communication networks to enhance troop mobility and logistics.
• This strategic push aims to improve connectivity to remote border areas and strengthen India's defensive capabilities.
• Emphasis is also on using advanced construction techniques suitable for challenging mountainous terrains.
• The move is seen as a response to evolving geopolitical dynamics and ensures robust border management.
gsSubTopics: Border areas and their management in security challenges and their management.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border infrastructure.
The Line of Actual Control (LAC) is the de facto border between India and China.
Daporijo bridge in Arunachal Pradesh and Atal Tunnel in Himachal Pradesh are key border infrastructure projects.
Infrastructure development is crucial for rapid troop deployment and supply lines.
The Border Area Development Programme (BADP) also contributes to infrastructure in border regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security, particularly along its northern frontiers.
Analyze the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, mountainous, and remote border areas.
Evaluate the impact of enhanced border connectivity on the socio-economic development of border populations.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India's accelerated border infrastructure development in the context of regional security dynamics.
Suggest measures to ensure the long-term sustainability and resilience of border infrastructure projects.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed strategic road winding through mountainous terrain in a border region.
GS3.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, formerly known as Rohtang Tunnel, is the longest highway single-tube tunnel above 10,000 feet (3,000 m) in the world.
